subject: UX & Research
title: The ADA and Digital Rights
description: A micro-lesson that makes The ADA and Digital Rights usable.

hook.text: The ADA became law in 1990. It guaranteed physical access. Digital access took decades longer.

buildup.text: Courts ruled the ADA covers digital spaces too. If your business is public, your site must be accessible.

discovery.text: Domino's was sued in 2019 for an inaccessible app. The Supreme Court let the case proceed.

twist.text: Web accessibility lawsuits tripled from 2017 to 2023. Legal risk now drives what ethics alone couldn't.

climax.text: The ADA didn't mention websites. But courts interpret it broadly. Digital is now clearly covered.

punchline.text: The ADA covers the web now. Access is a legal right, not a favor.

quiz.question: How has the ADA evolved regarding digital accessibility?
options: Courts ruled it applies to websites and apps, even though the original law didn't mention them
The ADA was updated in 2020 to add web-specific rules
The ADA only applies to government websites
